name: Shahrbanu
title: Mother of Imam Sajjad
shortDescription: Persian princess, wife of Imam Hussain (AS), and mother of Imam Sajjad (AS) who created a deep bond between Iranians and the Ahl al-Bayt.
fullStory:
Shahrbanu (or Shahzanan) was the daughter of Yazdegerd III (the last Sassanid king) who was brought to Medina as a prisoner of war. Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) treated her with respect, and she chose to marry Imam Hussain (AS) of her own free will.

The fruit of this blessed marriage was the birth of Ali ibn al-Hussain, Imam Sajjad (AS). Through this union, Shahrbanu created a deep and historical bond between Iranians and the Prophet's (PBUH) family.

Most authentic historians believe that Shahrbanu died of postpartum illness shortly after Imam Sajjad's (AS) birth and was not present in Karbala. However, in some folk legends and Ta'zieh (passion plays), stories are told of her presence in Karbala, riding the Imam's horse (Zuljanah) after Ashura, and taking refuge in the 'Bibi Shahrbanu' mountain in Rey.

Although her presence in Karbala is not historically proven, her name always shines as one of the great ladies and mother of an infallible Imam in Shia history and Iranian Ashura culture.
quote: (Imam Sajjad's pride:) I am the son of the two chosen ones; from the Arabs, Quraysh, and from the non-Arabs, Persians.
